option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# This line is needed on some machines. MAKE=make
# Set MUD_LIB to the directory which contains the mud data. Was formerly # defined in config.h ! MUD_LIB ?= $(HOME)/mudlib
# Set BINDIR to the directory where you want to install the executables. BINDIR ?= $(HOME)/mudbin
# Specify the interface source files on the ISRC line, and the corresponding # object files on the IOBJ line. # NB. interface.c should be edited accordingly. ISRC= clib/efun.c clib/stdobject.c clib/gl_language.c IOBJ= clib/efun.o clib/stdobject.o clib/gl_language.o
# List of objects that might not exist in your standard library # Select from: drand48.[co], strerror.[co], strpbrk.[co], strtod.[co], # snprintf.[co], vprintf.[co] MISSING_SRC=drand48.c snprintf.c MISSING_OBJ=drand48.o snprintf.o
# Chose one of these mallocs: # This is the standard malloc for the CD gamedriver. It is quite fast # and has a very small memory overhead. MALLOC=bibopmalloc
# This is the old standard mud malloc. It's a bit faster that bibopmalloc # but uses more memory. #MALLOC=smalloc
# A variant of smalloc for debug purposes #MALLOC=debugmalloc #DEBUG_MALLOC=-DDEBUG_MALLOC
# Gnu malloc, fastest but uses most memory. #MALLOC=gmalloc
# Use the standard malloc on your system: #MALLOC=sysmalloc
# Another debug allocator #MALLOC=dmalloc
# Define what random number generator to use. # If no one is specified, a guaranteed bad one will be used. # RAND=DRAND48
# Select a parser generator among the following: # The Berkely yacc. Produces the best code. Is available for anonymous ftp. #YACC=byacc
# Bison from the FSF. Produces good code. Is available for anonymous ftp. #YACC=bison -y
# Standard yacc. Should be available on any Unix system worth its salt. # WARNING: This will probably not work on all systems! YACC=yacc
# If you don't have 'strchr', then add next flag to CFLAGS. # -Dstrchr=index -Dstrrchr=rindex # PROFIL= #PROFIL=-p -DMARK #PROFIL=-pg #PROFIL=-pg -non_shared #Enable warnings from the compiler, if wanted. WARN=-Wall -Wshadow -Wstrict-prototypes #WARN= # # Enable run time debugging. It will use more time and space. # When the flag is changed, be sure to recompile everything. # Simply comment out this line if not wanted. # If you change anything in the source, you are strongly encouraged to have # DEBUG defined. # If you will not change anything, you are still strongly to have it defined, # as long as the game is not bug free. #DEBUG= DEBUG=-DDEBUG -ggdb # #
# System dependent flags # VAX BSD gcc #SYS_CFLAGS=-DVAX_BSD #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# OpenBSD (All platforms) #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g -O3 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# NetBSD or FreeBSD #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS=-lcrypt #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# Linux # Some versions might require -lcrypt added to SYS_LIBS SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe -D__USE_BSD_SIGNAL SYS_OPT=-O1 -fomit-frame-pointer SYS_LIBS=-lcrypt CC=/usr/bin/gcc ULIBS= LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# BSD/OS 2.x, 3.x and 4.x #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# SunOS 4 gcc #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS=-lresolv #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# SunOS 5 gcc #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe -DSOLARIS #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS=-lsocket -lnsl #CC=purify cc -DSOLARIS #CC=gcc -DSOLARIS -g #CC=gcc -O -Wall -g #ULIBS=-lresolv -lsocket -lnsl #LINTFILTER=sedf.sysv
# DEC Alpha cc #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g3 -O2 -Olimit 3000 #SYS_LIB= #CC=cc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# Irix #SYS_CFLAGS=-DSOLARIS #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sysv
CFLAGS= $(SYS_CFLAGS) $(SYS_OPT) -DWORD_WRAP -D$(RAND) -DMALLOC_$(MALLOC) \ $(WARN) $(DEBUG) $(DEBUG_MALLOC) LINTFLAGS= $(CFLAGS)
# # Add extra libraries here. # LIBS= -lm $(SYS_LIBS) MFLAGS = "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)"
driver: DUMMY $(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS) $(PROFIL)" "YACC=$(YACC)" "LIBS=$(LIBS)" "ISRC=$(ISRC)" "IOBJ=$(IOBJ)" "MALLOC=$(MALLOC)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)" "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" "MISSING_SRC=$(MISSING_SRC)" "MISSING_OBJ=$(MISSING_OBJ)" $@
install.utils: (cd util;\ $(MAKE) $(MFLAGS) "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LIBS=$(ULIBS)" install)
utils: (cd util;\ $(MAKE) $(MFLAGS) "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LIBS=$(ULIBS)")
install: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" $@
tags: TAGS
clean depend lint TAGS: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LINTFLAGS=$(LINTFLAGS)" "LINTFILTER=$(LINTFILTER)" "YACC=$(YACC)" "LIBS=$(LIBS)" "ISRC=$(ISRC)" "MALLOC=$(MALLOC)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)" "MISSING_SRC=$(MISSING_SRC)" $@
check: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib $@
DUMMY: mkdir -p $(BINDIR) $(MUD_LIB) || true
all: driver utils

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# This line is needed on some machines. MAKE=make
# Set MUD_LIB to the directory which contains the mud data. Was formerly # defined in config.h ! MUD_LIB ?= $(HOME)/mudlib
# Set BINDIR to the directory where you want to install the executables. BINDIR ?= $(HOME)/mudbin
# Specify the interface source files on the ISRC line, and the corresponding # object files on the IOBJ line. # NB. interface.c should be edited accordingly. ISRC= clib/efun.c clib/stdobject.c clib/gl_language.c IOBJ= clib/efun.o clib/stdobject.o clib/gl_language.o
# List of objects that might not exist in your standard library # Select from: drand48.[co], strerror.[co], strpbrk.[co], strtod.[co], # snprintf.[co], vprintf.[co] MISSING_SRC=drand48.c snprintf.c MISSING_OBJ=drand48.o snprintf.o
# Chose one of these mallocs: # This is the standard malloc for the CD gamedriver. It is quite fast # and has a very small memory overhead. MALLOC=bibopmalloc
# This is the old standard mud malloc. It's a bit faster that bibopmalloc # but uses more memory. #MALLOC=smalloc
# A variant of smalloc for debug purposes #MALLOC=debugmalloc #DEBUG_MALLOC=-DDEBUG_MALLOC
# Gnu malloc, fastest but uses most memory. #MALLOC=gmalloc
# Use the standard malloc on your system: #MALLOC=sysmalloc
# Another debug allocator #MALLOC=dmalloc
# Define what random number generator to use. # If no one is specified, a guaranteed bad one will be used. # RAND=DRAND48
# Select a parser generator among the following: # The Berkely yacc. Produces the best code. Is available for anonymous ftp. #YACC=byacc
# Bison from the FSF. Produces good code. Is available for anonymous ftp. #YACC=bison -y
# Standard yacc. Should be available on any Unix system worth its salt. # WARNING: This will probably not work on all systems! YACC=yacc
# If you don't have 'strchr', then add next flag to CFLAGS. # -Dstrchr=index -Dstrrchr=rindex # PROFIL= #PROFIL=-p -DMARK #PROFIL=-pg #PROFIL=-pg -non_shared #Enable warnings from the compiler, if wanted. WARN=-Wall -Wshadow -Wstrict-prototypes #WARN= # # Enable run time debugging. It will use more time and space. # When the flag is changed, be sure to recompile everything. # Simply comment out this line if not wanted. # If you change anything in the source, you are strongly encouraged to have # DEBUG defined. # If you will not change anything, you are still strongly to have it defined, # as long as the game is not bug free. #DEBUG= DEBUG=-DDEBUG -ggdb # #
# System dependent flags # VAX BSD gcc #SYS_CFLAGS=-DVAX_BSD #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# OpenBSD (All platforms) #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g -O3 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# NetBSD or FreeBSD #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS=-lcrypt #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# Linux # Some versions might require -lcrypt added to SYS_LIBS SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe -D__USE_BSD_SIGNAL SYS_OPT=-O1 -fomit-frame-pointer SYS_LIBS=-lcrypt CC=/usr/bin/gcc ULIBS= LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# BSD/OS 2.x, 3.x and 4.x #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# SunOS 4 gcc #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS=-lresolv #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# SunOS 5 gcc #SYS_CFLAGS=-pipe -DSOLARIS #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS=-lsocket -lnsl #CC=purify cc -DSOLARIS #CC=gcc -DSOLARIS -g #CC=gcc -O -Wall -g #ULIBS=-lresolv -lsocket -lnsl #LINTFILTER=sedf.sysv
# DEC Alpha cc #SYS_CFLAGS= #SYS_OPT=-g3 -O2 -Olimit 3000 #SYS_LIB= #CC=cc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sun
# Irix #SYS_CFLAGS=-DSOLARIS #SYS_OPT=-g -O2 #SYS_LIBS= #CC=gcc #ULIBS= #LINTFILTER=sedf.sysv
CFLAGS= $(SYS_CFLAGS) $(SYS_OPT) -DWORD_WRAP -D$(RAND) -DMALLOC_$(MALLOC) \ $(WARN) $(DEBUG) $(DEBUG_MALLOC) LINTFLAGS= $(CFLAGS)
# # Add extra libraries here. # LIBS= -lm $(SYS_LIBS) MFLAGS = "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)"
driver: DUMMY $(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS) $(PROFIL)" "YACC=$(YACC)" "LIBS=$(LIBS)" "ISRC=$(ISRC)" "IOBJ=$(IOBJ)" "MALLOC=$(MALLOC)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)" "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" "MISSING_SRC=$(MISSING_SRC)" "MISSING_OBJ=$(MISSING_OBJ)" $@
install.utils: (cd util;\ $(MAKE) $(MFLAGS) "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LIBS=$(ULIBS)" install)
utils: (cd util;\ $(MAKE) $(MFLAGS) "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LIBS=$(ULIBS)")
install: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "BINDIR=$(BINDIR)" $@
tags: TAGS
clean depend lint TAGS: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib "CC=$(CC)" "CFLAGS=$(CFLAGS)" "LINTFLAGS=$(LINTFLAGS)" "LINTFILTER=$(LINTFILTER)" "YACC=$(YACC)" "LIBS=$(LIBS)" "ISRC=$(ISRC)" "MALLOC=$(MALLOC)" "MUD_LIB=$(MUD_LIB)" "MISSING_SRC=$(MISSING_SRC)" $@
check: -$(MAKE) -f Makefile.distrib $@
DUMMY: mkdir -p $(BINDIR) $(MUD_LIB) || true
all: driver utils
